prefs.wipeWarningBody: Resetting wipes this device clean — every wallet, your password, and Face ID all go away, and Aura begins fresh. Confirm your seed phrases are saved somewhere safe, because there's no way to reverse this.
prefs.holdingLockIntroPara: Encrypting your storage layers on extra protection, locking down how your data sits on this device so that nobody can reach your information without your say-so.
prefs.holdingLockCaveatPara: Keep in mind, though, that this only guards entry to the wallets held in your device keychain. The wallets themselves get no separate password or added safeguard from it.
prefs.passcodeHint: Choose the password that will unlock your storage.
prefs.unlockHoldingQuestion: Decrypt your storage? Once you do, your wallets can be opened with no password at all.
prefs.quoteRetrievalFailed: We couldn't fetch the rate for the currency you picked.
prefs.badLinkWarning: That URL won't work. Please type a valid one that begins with http:// or https://.
prefs.safeguardKeysReminder: Never forget to back up your keys!
prefs.relaunchForDirection: Aura needs to restart before the new language direction is applied.

onboarding.valueProposition: Hold your own Bitcoin keys and handle your coins with complete privacy and safety. Entirely non-custodial.
onboarding.transparencyNote: Open-source, public, and auditable code over on GitHub.

holdings.cosignedBlurb: for big balances or shared control
holdings.restoreInstructions: Type in your recovery phrase or a private key (WIF). Aura figures out the format and brings in your wallet.
holdings.schemeSwitchNote: Switches the address scheme this wallet receives on, along with its extended public key (xpub) and the Addresses list. Your balance and transaction history stay the same.
holdings.noActivityYet: Transactions you make will show up here.
holdings.qrTransferHint: Scan this QR code to bring your wallet into another app.
holdings.stashForRecovery: Copy this and stash it somewhere safe so you can restore your wallet down the road.
holdings.restoreFailure: Import didn't work. Double-check that the data you entered is valid.
holdings.penAndPaperNote: Jot down these words and keep them somewhere safe. They let you restore your wallet later on.

seedBackup.savePhraseBeforeProceeding: Back up your phrase before continuing
seedBackup.mnemonicCrucialNotice:
Recording your recovery phrase matters — without it there's no way back to your wallet if this device is lost.
Look it over closely, then confirm once more.

restore.cameraCaptureLink: or scan a QR code instead?
restore.optionalSecretWordHint: Add your passphrase if you set one.

appGeneral.mnemonicCheckFailed: That's not a valid BIP39 mnemonic. Review the words and give it another go.
appGeneral.coinUnitHint: The unit every Bitcoin amount is shown in.
appGeneral.tactileResponseHint: A gentle buzz acknowledges your taps and actions.
appGeneral.optOutTrackingHint: Anonymous performance and diagnostic data stays off the record and unshared.
appGeneral.mempoolFallbackHint: If your Electrum servers fail, fall back to mempool.space. This can link your addresses to your IP, so it's off by default.

outflow.fundsShortReduceAmount: Balance is too low. Send a smaller amount or trim the fee.
outflow.mockupOnlyNotice: This send screen is just a design preview.
outflow.emptyPickHolding: No spendable coins are picked in this wallet.
outflow.emptyPickWallet: No spendable coins are picked in this wallet.
outflow.overSpendableLockedExcluded: That's more than your available balance. Keep in mind frozen coins don't count.
outflow.overWalletHoldings: That goes beyond the wallet balance.
outflow.minerCostBeatsValue: The network fee outweighs the amount.
outflow.belowDustThreshold: After the fee, fewer than 546 sats would remain to send.
outflow.nothingSpendable: There are no UTXOs available to spend.
outflow.minOnePayeeNeeded: You need at least one recipient.
outflow.payeeValuePairingOff: Every recipient needs exactly one amount.
outflow.valueUnderFloorSats: That amount is below the minimum. Enter something above 500 sats.
outflow.sumOverHoldingsLockedExcluded: What you're sending tops your available balance. Remember that frozen coins are left out.
outflow.sumOverHoldings: What you're sending tops your available balance.
outflow.cameraGrantRequired: Camera access is needed before we can continue.
outflow.payeeFinishBeforeAdding: Finish filling in recipient #{number} before you add another one.

quorum.cosignerReadyBackup: Your Wallet key is ready. Pause now and back up your mnemonic seed somewhere safe.
quorum.restoreFromSeed: Paste in a seed to bring back your existing Wallet key.

qrScan.decodeFromPhoto: Scan a QR from an image
qrScan.lensModuleMissing: This build doesn't include the camera module yet. Run `pod install` and rebuild on a real device to turn on scanning.

nodeConn.publishDuplicate: This transaction was already sent out.
nodeConn.publishBadReply: The server sent back an invalid response.
nodeConn.publishTimedOut: The broadcast ran out of time before the server answered.
nodeConn.publishHostUnavailable: We couldn't reach the broadcast server.
nodeConn.disconnectedBlurb: Stay disconnected and skip every network lookup.
nodeConn.tipReachedNotice: Up to date with the latest block.
nodeConn.customPeerBlurb: Point the wallet at your own server. Only public addresses and signed transactions ever travel over the connection.
nodeConn.hostBlankDetail: Enter a server host before saving.
nodeConn.hostMalformedDetail: That host doesn't look right. Use a domain such as server.example.com.
nodeConn.leftoverFormatNote: Choose which address format receives the change from your transactions. Automatic keeps the change in the same format as the wallet you are spending from. Your keys stay on this device; only public addresses are derived here.
nodeConn.chargeBelowZeroWarning: Enter a fee rate greater than zero.
nodeConn.chargeGuidanceNote: Pick how quickly you want transactions to confirm, or set your own rate in satoshis per virtual byte. Suggested rates are read from the public mempool; nothing about your wallet is shared to fetch them.

guard.cipherActivationFailed: Encryption couldn't be turned on. Give it another try.
guard.identityCheckBlurb: {type} confirms it's you ahead of anything sensitive — opening Aura, sending a transaction, exporting a wallet, or removing one.
guard.passphraseGateBlurb: Lock your wallets behind a password that's yours alone.
guard.hiddenHoldingBlurb: Set up a decoy password that unlocks a separate, empty wallet.
guard.holdingReadFailurePrompt: We couldn't read your encrypted storage. Reset it? You can bring wallets back from your recovery phrase.

seedRecovery.writeWordsInOrderGuidance: Record these {count} words in the right order and stash them somewhere safe. They're your only route back to this wallet.

holdingItem.freeUnlimitedCreation: It costs nothing, and you can make as many as you want.

utxoControl.pickingHint: Pick coins to freeze them, or to spend only those on your next payment. Tap a coloured circle to begin selecting.

contactDirectory.heading: Address Book
contactDirectory.vacantPlaceholder: Nothing saved yet — addresses you pay land here.

camScan.galleryDecodeUnsupportedBody: Reading a QR from a saved image calls for an app rebuild (pod install).
camScan.codeMissingBody: There's no readable QR code in that image.
camScan.imageLoadFailedBody: Give a different image a try.

ossInfo.keypairCreationBlurb: React Native and bitcoinjs-lib create your keys, all in line with the BIP-39 standard.
ossInfo.mnemonicStretchBlurb: PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA512 run over 2,048 iterations — precisely what the BIP-39 standard calls for.
ossInfo.localKeyAssuranceHeading: Your keys stay on your phone, always
ossInfo.localKeyAssuranceBlurb: Aura looks at the blockchain through public Electrum servers of your choosing — no accounts, no Aura servers, no analytics.
ossInfo.auditableRepoBlurb: All of Aura's code sits in a single public repository. Read it, audit it, and confirm for yourself exactly what your wallet does — how keys come to be, how your seed is derived, and that Aura can never lay eyes on your funds.

decoyMode.coercionExplanation: There are moments when someone might pressure you into giving up your password. To shield your coins, Aura can set up a second storage, separately encrypted with its own password. If you're ever cornered, hand over that one instead: typing it into Aura opens a decoy storage that looks fully real to onlookers, while your real storage — and the coins inside it — stays out of sight and protected.
decoyMode.holdingSetupError: Storage couldn't be created. Give it another try.

pwPrompt.decoyHoldingKeyGuidance: Pick the password you'll use to unlock your storage. The fake storage password must differ from the one on your main storage.
pwPrompt.wrongSecretRetry: That password's wrong. Please try again.

alerts.tagline: Get pinged about incoming payments and confirmations.
alerts.overviewBlurb: Aura lets you know the moment coins land or a transaction confirms. Switch it off whenever you like.
alerts.relayTokenDisclosure: With notifications on, Aura passes this device's push token along with your wallets' addresses and transaction IDs to the relay server so it can ping you.
alerts.forwardOnlyScope: Only what happens after you switch notifications on gets sent — nothing from before is ever picked up.
alerts.serverPurgeNote: Switching notifications off, or removing a wallet, wipes that wallet's data from the server.
alerts.settlementHint: Ping me when a payment confirms.
alerts.sampleReachableHint: Confirm that alerts make it to this device.
alerts.sampleBlockedHint: Switch on notifications to run a test.
alerts.devicePermissionsHint: Handle notification permissions over in iOS.

aboutApp.communityProjectStatement: Aura is a free, open-source project. Made by people who use Bitcoin.

addrBook.proofToolExplainer: From here you can produce or check a cryptographic signature tied to a Bitcoin address.

coinSelect.noUtxosNotice: There aren't any coins in this wallet right now.

stealthHolding.decoyRealismTip: The new storage works just like the real one, and you can keep a small amount in it to make the decoy more convincing.
stealthHolding.duplicatePassphraseWarning: That password is already taken. Pick a different one.
